stage.name: XRPL Deep Dive
stage.description: RPCA consensus, trust lines, native DEX + AMM, pathfinding, ODL, and why OnlyCrypto runs on XRPL.

elements.content: How XRPL achieves 3–5 second finality with no miners

Propose

Any validator proposes a candidate set of transactions

Threshold

80% of trusted validators must agree on the same set

Apply

Agreed transactions applied to ledger state simultaneously

Close

Ledger version closed with new state hash and sequence number

Finality

3–5 seconds: irreversible. No forks. No reorganizations.

actions.text: 80%. Required threshold
RPCA Consensus
How XRPL achieves 3–5 second finality with no miners
ProposeAny validator proposes a candidate set of transactions
Threshold80% of trusted validators must agree on the same set
ApplyAgreed transactions applied to ledger state simultaneously
CloseLedger version closed with new state hash and sequence number
Finality3–5 seconds: irreversible. No forks. No reorganizations

elements.content: XRPL's permission system for tokens and credit

Trust Lines

You cannot receive an IOUtoken on XRPL unless you explicitly create a trust line to the issuer.

Trust line = bilateral agreement: max credit, freeze rights, rippling settings.

This prevents spam tokens appearing in wallets without consent — unlike ERC-20 tokens that can be airdropped to anyone.

actions.text: Trust Lines & Rippling

XRPL's permission system for tokens and credit. Trust LinesYou cannot receive an IOUtoken on XRPL unless you explicitly create a trust line to the issuer. . Trust line = bilateral agreement: max credit, freeze rights, rippling settings. . This prevents spam tokens appearing in wallets without consent — unlike ERC-20 tokens that can be airdropped to anyone

elements.content: XRPL's permission system for tokens and credit

Rippling

Rippling = allow your IOUs to be passed through to other accounts that trust the same issuer.

Example: Alice trusts USD/Bank. Bob trusts USD/Bank. Alice can send Bob USD without Bank moving any actual money — the ledger adjusts IOU balances.

Disable rippling on personal accounts — only issuers need it.

elements.content: Protocol-level trading — no smart contracts, no external risk

Native Order Book DEX

XRPL has a fully functional order book built into the ledger protocol — no smart contract needed.

Any two assets with a common bridge currency can be traded.

Offers (orders) live on-ledger. Auto-bridging via XRP automatically routes through XRP when it improves the rate.

actions.text: XRPL Native DEX + AMM

Protocol-level trading — no smart contracts, no external risk. Native Order Book DEXXRPL has a fully functional order book built into the ledger protocol — no smart contract needed. . Any two assets with a common bridge currency can be traded. . Offers (orders) live on-ledger. Auto-bridging via XRP automatically routes through XRP when it improves the rate

elements.content: Protocol-level trading — no smart contracts, no external risk

AMM (Added 2024)

XRPL AMM Proposal (XLS-30) went live 2024.

Constant-function AMM alongside the order book. LPs deposit both sides, earn trading fees.

Continuous Auction Mechanism (CAM): fee rises during high volatility to compensate LPs for IL — first AMM to implement this automatically.

elements.content: How XRPL routes payments across currencies in seconds

XRPL pathfinding finds the best route across multiple currencies and order books automatically.

ODL (On-Demand Liquidity) uses XRP as a bridge:
Sender deposits USD → XRP purchased → XRP sent to Mexico → XRP sold → recipient receives MXN
Total time: 3–5 seconds. No pre-funded accounts needed.
RippleNet ODL processed $15B+ in cross-border payments in 2023. Traditional SWIFT settlement: 2–5 business days.

elements.content:
XRPL

• 1 XRP account reserve (was 20, voted down)

• 0.0002 XRP per transaction (burned)

• 3–5 second finality, no forks

• 1,500 tx/s native (50K+ with AMM)

• No smart contracts = no contract bugs

• Leaderless consensus — no mining pools

Ethereum

• No account reserve needed

• $1–$20+ gas fees (variable)

• ~12 second blocks, reorg risk exists

• ~15 tx/s L1 (100K+ via L2s)

• Smart contracts = programmable but risky

• 33 validators control >50% of stake

actions.text: XRPL vs Ethereum
Different design choices, different trade-offs

XRPL• 1 XRP account reserve (was 20, voted down)• 0.0002 XRP per transaction (burned)• 3–5 second finality, no forks• 1,500 tx/s native (50K+ with AMM)• No smart contracts = no contract bugs• Leaderless consensus — no mining pools

Ethereum• No account reserve needed• $1–$20+ gas fees (variable)• ~12 second blocks, reorg risk exists• ~15 tx/s L1 (100K+ via L2s)• Smart contracts = programmable but risky• 33 validators control >50% of stake

elements.content:
XRPL Hooks (Coming)

Hooks = lightweight logic attached to accounts — triggered on send/receive.

Unlike Ethereum's full EVM, Hooks are deliberately limited: no loops, no storage, deterministic execution.

Use cases: auto-convert received XRP to RLUSD, block incoming transactions from unknown senders, auto-route to savings.

actions.text: XRPL's Roadmap

Hooks, sidechains, and what's coming next. XRPL Hooks (Coming)Hooks = lightweight logic attached to accounts — triggered on send/receive. . Unlike Ethereum's full EVM, Hooks are deliberately limited: no loops, no storage, deterministic execution. . Use cases: auto-convert received XRP to RLUSD, block incoming transactions from unknown senders, auto-route to savings

scenes.title: Why OnlyCrypto Runs on XRPL (Part B)

elements.content:
Federated Sidechains

XRPL can bridge to EVM-compatible sidechains (XRPL EVM Sidechain).

Solidity contracts run on the sidechain, settle to XRPL mainnet.

Best of both worlds: Ethereum ecosystem tools (MetaMask, Hardhat) with XRPL's speed and cost for final settlement.

actions.text: XRPL's Roadmap (Part B)

Hooks, sidechains, and what's coming next. Federated SidechainsXRPL can bridge to EVM-compatible sidechains (XRPL EVM Sidechain). . Solidity contracts run on the sidechain, settle to XRPL mainnet. . Best of both worlds: Ethereum ecosystem tools (MetaMask, Hardhat) with XRPL's speed and cost for final settlement

elements.content: How every member payment flows through the ledger

Member Pays

Member sends XRP to OnlyCrypto hot wallet with destination tag identifying their account

Watcher Detects

XRPL watcher confirms tx on-ledger: amount, tag, finality in <5 seconds

DB Updated

Supabase updated: subscription activated, payout queue populated

Payouts Sent

XRPL sends referral commissions, rank bonuses, trader payouts — all in XRP, all on-chain

Cost

$0.0002 total in fees. 0 chargebacks. 0 bank delays. Fully auditable on-chain.

elements.content: How XRP solves the pre-funding problem in global payments

Traditional cross-border payments require banks to pre-fund accounts in every currency corridor — locking up trillions of dollars in idle capital. XRP solves this as a real-time bridge currency.


Flow: USD → XRP → PHP, all in 3–5 seconds. No pre-funded nostro accounts needed. The liquidity is sourced from market makers who hold XRP. Ripple calls this On-Demand Liquidity (ODL) — used commercially by MoneyGram, SBI Remit, and 50+ partners.

actions.text: 🌉 XRP as Bridge Currency
How XRP solves the pre-funding problem in global payments

Traditional cross-border payments require banks to pre-fund accounts in every currency corridor — locking up trillions of dollars in idle capital. XRP solves this as a real-time bridge currency. Flow: USD → XRP → PHP, all in 3–5 seconds. No pre-funded nostro accounts needed. The liquidity is sourced from market makers who hold XRP. Ripple calls this On-Demand Liquidity (ODL) — used commercially by MoneyGram, SBI Remit, and 50+ partners
🏦 Pre-Funding. Trillions locked in nostro accounts
⚡ ODL. USD → XRP → target currency
⏱️ 3-5 Seconds. Full settlement, any corridor
💰 Savings. Eliminates pre-funded capital requirement

elements.content:
Asset Tokenization on XRPL — the ledger supports native token issuance via trust lines. CBDCs, stablecoins, tokenized securities, and RWAs can be issued and traded on XRPL's native DEX. Multiple central banks are piloting XRPL for CBDC issuance.


Micropayments — XRPL's sub-cent fees make it viable for content monetization at micro-scale: pay $0.001 per article read, stream $0.0001 per second to a creator, tip a musician $0.05 in real-time. Use cases impossible with traditional payment rails.

🪙 Native Tokens
Issue any asset via trust lines

💸 Micropayments
<$0.01 fees enable new business models

Asset Tokenization on XRPL — the ledger supports native token issuance via trust lines. CBDCs, stablecoins, tokenized securities, and RWAs can be issued and traded on XRPL's native DEX. Multiple central banks are piloting XRPL for CBDC issuance. Micropayments — XRPL's sub-cent fees make it viable for content monetization at micro-scale: pay $0.001 per article read, stream $0.0001 per second to a creator, tip a musician $0.05 in real-time. questions.question: XRPL's RPCA consensus requires what percentage of validators to agree for a transaction to be finalized?
questions.analysis: RPCA requires 80% of trusted validators to agree. This gives strong Byzantine fault tolerance while maintaining high throughput.

questions.question: What is an XRPL Trust Line?

options.label: A payment channel between two accounts
An explicit permission to hold a non-XRP token issued by a specific issuer
A smart contract for token swaps
The validator's reputation score

questions.analysis: Trust lines are XRPL's permission system — you must explicitly trust an issuer before receiving their token. This prevents spam tokens from appearing in wallets.

questions.question: What is the minimum XRP reserve required to activate an XRPL account (as of 2024)?

options.label: 20 XRP
10 XRP
1 XRP
0.5 XRP

questions.analysis: The reserve was reduced from 20 XRP to 10 XRP, then to 1 XRP via validator governance votes. Always verify the current reserve with a live ServerInfo call.

questions.question: Which TWO are unique features of XRPL's native DEX (not found in most EVM DEXes)?

options.label: Automatic market maker (AMM) pools
Built-in order book at the protocol layer (no smart contract needed)
Pathfinding that auto-routes through multiple currencies
Flash loans on any token pair

questions.analysis: XRPL has a native order book ledger object — no smart contract required. Its pathfinding engine auto-routes through intermediate currencies for best execution. AMMs exist on XRPL too but also exist on EVM chains.

questions.question: OnlyCrypto uses XRPL for member payments. Which answer gives three specific technical reasons why XRPL is better suited for this use case than Ethereum?

options.label: XRPL settles in 3–5 seconds with $0.0002 fees and 1,500 tx/s natively; Ethereum takes ~12 seconds with $1–$20 fees at ~15 tx/s L1 — making XRPL far cheaper and faster for high-volume micro-payouts
XRPL supports smart contracts, unlimited token issuance, and EVM compatibility — all advantages over Ethereum for payment use cases
Ethereum has no transaction fees and settles instantly, but XRPL is chosen because it is newer and more modern technology
XRPL requires 20 XRP reserve per account making it more secure, while Ethereum's gas fees make it cheaper for large transfers

questions.analysis: Three technical reasons: (1) 3–5 second finality vs Ethereum's ~12 second blocks with reorg risk. (2) $0.0002 fixed fee vs $1–$20 variable gas — critical for paying many members small amounts. (3) 1,500 tx/s native vs ~15 tx/s Ethereum L1 — XRPL handles volume without congestion. The XRPL comparison slide in this lesson covers all three.

questions.question: What is a 'trust line' on the XRP Ledger?

options.label: A validator reputation score used in RPCA consensus
An account setting that allows holding non-XRP tokens up to a set limit
A cross-chain bridge between XRPL and Ethereum
A smart contract for automated payments

questions.analysis: Trust lines are XRPL's mechanism for holding issued currencies. To hold USDC on XRPL, you create a trust line to the USDC issuer allowing up to your chosen limit. The issuer can't send you more than your limit. This prevents unwanted token receipt and makes XRPL's token system permission-based.

questions.question: How does XRPL's native DEX differ from Uniswap?

options.label: XRPL DEX uses a bonding curve while Uniswap uses an order book
XRPL DEX is built into the ledger protocol with a native order book — no smart contract layer
XRPL DEX only supports XRP pairs while Uniswap supports any ERC-20
XRPL DEX uses proof of work for trade settlement

questions.analysis: XRPL's DEX is consensus-layer functionality — every node processes it natively. No smart contract deployment required. This eliminates MEV sandwich attacks (order execution is deterministic in consensus), reduces attack surface, and allows the AMM and order book to combine liquidity in a single trade path.

questions.question: What is XRPL's pathfinding and why is it powerful?

options.label: A routing algorithm that finds the cheapest gas path for transactions
A protocol that atomically converts through intermediate currencies to settle in the recipient's currency
A bridge protocol connecting XRPL to Ethereum
An algorithm that selects which validators process each transaction

questions.analysis: XRPL pathfinding enables one transaction to route through multiple currency conversions atomically. Sending USD to someone who wants EUR might route USD → XRP → EUR in a single transaction. All steps succeed or all fail — no partial execution risk. This is the infrastructure for ODL and global real-time settlement.
